name: Chicken Sauce Piquante
recipeInstructions:
1. Season chicken pieces liberally with salt and pepper.
2. Melt 1/4 cup vegetable shortening in a large Dutch oven and sauté the chicken until brown on all sides.
3. Add water, celery (minced), green bell pepper (chopped), green onions (chopped), and bay leaves to the pot. Simmer the sauce.
4. In a separate saucepan, melt 3/4 cup vegetable shortening. Gradually add flour, stirring constantly until dark brown.
5. Stir in minced onions and garlic; cook until translucent.
6. Add tomato paste and simmer until thickened.
7. Pour the flour mixture into the Dutch oven with the chicken. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and let simmer for 2 to 2 1/2 hours, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
8. If the sauce becomes too thick before the chicken is fully cooked, add water as needed, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
9. During the last 30 minutes of cooking, stir in mushrooms, optional green olives (chopped), lemon slices or seeds, parsley, and cayenne pepper for heat.
10. Just before serving, add white wine and stir gently.
11. Serve hot over spaghetti or cooked rice.
